notes: Codex maintains a single-session task list via the update_plan tool (full-list replace, steps each pending|in_progress|completed). Driver asks codex to track three steps and walk each pending→in_progress→completed. The lifecycle arc (ready→working→ready) and SubagentCount=0 are observable and asserted as phases. KNOWN FAILING: the load-bearing tasks-content observable — three items in the session API tasks field with matching subjects + status — is NOT produced because the codex parser routes update_plan through the generic function_call path and emits no TaskDelta/TaskSnapshot (the local transcript trace exists and carries plan[].step/status, so this is a parser bug, not an architectural incapability). The spec asserts the CORRECT behavior; record files the issue and records known_failing.

text: Rollout session appears in ready (first phase anchored UNPINNED to start so a transient proc-<PID> presession can't steal birth)
User prompt drives the session ready → working (no delay bound — codex turn-start latency is unbounded)

invariants: no state_transition to waiting for session
no state_transition to ready for session
text: Session stays working across the update_plan calls — the tool auto-executes (no permission gate), so no waiting fires and no premature ready dip; no child sessions spawn (SubagentCount stays 0)
task_complete drives working → ready at turn end; final session state is ready; no child sessions were spawned
